story: Tobu's original father — brought the plushie to life and started the whole tradition.
commentary: Brought a stuffed bull into a WhatsApp group and appointed himself father by default. Democracy skipped this one entirely.

story: Crushed her story-telling speech in the Aula Magna — crowned queen of Tobu by popular acclaim.
commentary: One good speech in the Aula Magna and the section handed her a crown like she had just closed a Series A. Solid five minutes, inflated title.

story: "Woman lovers" — 44 votes. The quote that launched a thousand reactions.
commentary: "Woman lovers" is barely a sentence and it still beat three other entries by 44 votes. The competition was not just weak, it forfeited.

story: Took Tobu on her first international trip, to Switzerland — Tobu's passport game started here.
commentary: Flew a stuffed bull to Switzerland like it had its own frequent flyer number. Extremely committed to a bit nobody made her commit to.

story: Misread Claire Linares' lunch invite, replied after the first sentence — then told the whole list: "next time I will consider actually reading the full email instead of replying after the first sentence."
commentary: Misread one email, replied after sentence one, then confessed the whole thing to the entire distribution list instead of quietly deleting it. Won for turning a mistake into a public spectacle.

story: Asked by Stephanie Felix to say something nice ("he said to say something nice!"), replied with a single, devastating: "ummmm."
commentary: Handed the easiest compliment in human history and answered with "ummmm." Choking under literally zero pressure, on the record.

story: Dropped the quote: "sometimes it's better not to speak it."
commentary: "Sometimes it is better not to speak it" sounds like it belongs on a fortune cookie or a strategy slide — either way, nobody in that room could tell if she had stumbled on genuine wisdom or just delivered really good corporate-speak for "I have nothing."

story: Asked what kind of business sells perishable goods, answered: "A pet shop." — 27 votes.
commentary: Technically defensible if you count the pets themselves as the perishable inventory. Bleak, but not actually wrong.

story: Delivered a carbon-credits statement so incomprehensible that Professor Fernando Peñalva simply moved past it. 29 votes.
commentary: Said something about carbon credits so tangled that Professor Peñalva just quietly abandoned the thread mid-class. A professor giving up is not a small thing.

story: Volunteered to explain a concept at the board; Alejandro offered his chalk, Joan ignored it and grabbed his own from beside the board instead. 39 votes.
commentary: Got handed the chalk directly by the professor and walked past it to grab his own, like it was beneath him. A flex nobody asked for and everyone noticed.

story: "The MBA is a sunk cost" — 39 votes. Delivered with the confidence of someone who has done the math.
commentary: Called the MBA a sunk cost, out loud, to a room of people who had paid the exact same fee. Bold, correct, and terrible for morale.

story: "I don't want to be your wife, Lasse. You can't forget your children for 9 months." — 32 votes.
commentary: Turned down a marriage proposal nobody made and somehow made rejecting it the plot of his entire week. Second Tobu, same theatrics.

story: Only nomination of the week — took Tobu on a world tour over the holidays. Tobu saw more countries than most MBAs.
commentary: Won unopposed — literally the only nomination — and took that as license to parade a stuffed bull through three countries.
